treatment_information.treatment_response: Pain significantly improved and tolerating a stage 4 bariatric diet

treatment_timeline.event: Patient experienced epigastric pain and chest pain radiating to left arm with associated diaphoresis
Taken to emergency department where symptoms improved with baby ASA, O2, and nitroglycerin
Transferral to another facility for further care due to need for GI workup
Abdominal CT scan performed, showing multiple ventral wall hernias
EGD was obtained, which was not revealing as a source of pain
Discharged home with improved condition and dietary instructions

patient_outcome.final_status: Ambulatory - Independent with significantly improved pain
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow-up with Hematologist for low iron saturation and ferritin recommended
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Mental status clear and coherent, alert and interactive
